text: The United Kingdom is currently a mess, but this isn't about whether or not the Prime Minister held an illegal Christmas party, breaking lockdown rules, or the other scandals that Boris Johnson's facing. I'm talking about something much, much bigger—a set of problems so systemic that they go beyond party politics and could affect the future of the United Kingdom. One week, supermarket shelves are empty; the next, there are fights at gas stations as the fuel runs dry. Benefits are being cut, taxes are going up, wages have stagnated for more than a decade, and the COVID death toll is rising. On top of that, there's growing momentum for Scotland and maybe even Northern Ireland to leave this mess behind and break away from the UK. So, why does it feel like the UK is falling apart, and what's caused this crisis?
